name: rn-observability
description: Logging, error messages, and debugging patterns for React Native. Use when adding logging, designing error messages, debugging production issues, or improving code observability.
capabilities: Generate structured logging functions with context objects
Design error messages with diagnostic information
Implement flow tracing for multi-step operations
Add state snapshots for debugging complex flows
author: CJHarmath
securitySummary: Pure documentation skill containing only markdown patterns and code examples. No executable code, no file system access, no network calls, no external commands. All 75 static findings are false positives caused by the scanner misinterpreting documentation syntax (backticks for code blocks, Object.keys() method calls, YAML frontmatter) as security threats. This is a documentation-only skill providing observability guidance.
valueStatement: Silent failures create debugging nightmares. Use logging patterns, error message design, and structured debugging techniques to diagnose production issues fast.
limitations: Does not execute code or access file systems
Does not connect to external logging services
Does not modify existing code automatically
Provides patterns only, user must implement

useCases.title: Add logging to new features
useCases.description: Generate observability code for new features with structured logging and proper error handling

useCases.title: Debug production issues
useCases.description: Apply debugging patterns to diagnose issues using logs, flow traces, and state snapshots

useCases.title: Design error messages
useCases.description: Create error messages that include diagnostic context for faster resolution

promptTemplates.title: Basic logging setup
promptTemplates.scenario: Add logging to a new React Native function
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate a structured logger for React Native with debug, info, warn, and error levels. Include a logging wrapper that respects environment. Use rn-observability patterns.

promptTemplates.title: Error handling
promptTemplates.prompt: Write error handling for a data fetch function using rn-observability patterns. Include function name, what failed, relevant state, and possible cause.

promptTemplates.title: Flow tracing
promptTemplates.prompt: Implement flow tracing for a multi-step operation in React Native. Use flow IDs, step logging, and error capturing. Follow rn-observability patterns.

promptTemplates.title: Secure logging
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a secure logging utility that redacts sensitive fields like passwords, tokens, and API keys before logging. Use rn-observability patterns.

bestPractices: Log every early return with why it returned and relevant context
Include function name, diagnostic state, and hypothesis in error messages
Use flow IDs to trace multi-step operations across async boundaries
Redact sensitive data before logging to prevent credential exposure
antiPatterns: Silent early returns without logging why execution stopped
Generic error messages without diagnostic context
Console.log without structured data for searching
Logging sensitive data like passwords, tokens, or API keys

faq.question: Which React Native versions are supported?
faq.answer: Patterns work with React Native 0.70+ using modern TypeScript and async/await patterns.

faq.question: What logging levels should I use?
faq.answer: Error for exceptions, warn for unexpected conditions, info for business events, debug for diagnostics.

faq.question: Can this integrate with Sentry or Crashlytics?
faq.answer: Yes. Use the captureError pattern to send errors to services while maintaining local logging.

faq.question: Is sensitive data safe with these patterns?
faq.answer: Yes when using the secureLogger pattern with redaction for keys like password, token, and apiKey.

faq.question: Why are my logs not appearing in production?
faq.answer: Check log levels. Production typically uses warn/error only. Use captureError for production visibility.

faq.question: How is this different from React Native Debugger?
faq.answer: This provides code patterns for persistent observability. Debugger is for interactive debugging sessions.
